 Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring 
Service Quality Information Document for 
Global yearly CO2 sink 
Issue : 6.0 
21.09.2023 
 
 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
4 
I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
GLOBAL_OMI_HEALTH_carbon_co2_flux_integrated 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The OMI global yearly ocean CO2 sink represents the ocean uptake of CO2 from the atmosphere 
computed over the whole ocean. It is computed by multiplying the 12-month running mean of 
the surface ocean downward CO 2 mass flux (fgco2 ) [ molC m -2 yr -1], a variable from the 
MULTIOBS_GLO_BIO_CARBON_SURFACE_REP_015_008 product, by the area of each grid cell, 
and then taking the sum over all cells. The unit is finally converted to PgC per year (Figure 1). 
The yearly estimate of the ocean CO2 sink corresponds to the mean of a 100-member ensemble 
(see the method described in Chau et al . (2022, 2023 ) in the QUID for 
GLOBAL_OMI_HEALTH_carbon_ph_area_averaged). In line with Friedlingstein et al. ( 2022), 
yearly uncertainties are estimated as their corresponding ensemble standard deviation (1σ). 
I.3 Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service  products used as 
input for the computations 
 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
MULTIOBS_GLO_BIO_CARBON_S
URFACE_REP_015_008

https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/docu
ments/QUID/CMEMS-MOB-QUID-015-008.pdf 
PUM: 
https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/docu
ments/PUM/CMEMS-MOB-PUM-015-008.pdf

II SCIENTIFIC EVALUATION 
 
 
Figure 1: Area-integrated yearly surface downward flux of total CO2 for 1985-2022. Plain line: mean of 
100 samples; range: empirical 68 % confidence interval (mean estimate ± standard deviation) 
computed from 100 samples.  Trend

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the Ocean Monitoring Indicator for global yearly ocean CO2 sink, detailing its computation method and scientific evaluation based on Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service products.
